pages.raw_text:
The city fathers and Corporation
Counsel Geraghty have professed
heretofore to believe that the water
power company would build a
bridge of their own across the river
and the city in time would have
to pay the whole expense of a new
structure.


This craven fear, which to a dis
interested public looked like bun,
combe and subterfuge, is now laid
to rest by the offer of the Graves
people to pay for the bridge which
the bluffing water power company
threaten to leave on the city's
hands.


Now there is absolutely no ex-
cuse for voting the taxpayers
money to build that bridge for the
Washington Water Power company.
II that monopoly refuses to use the
city's bridge because it lie required
to build a new structure, In keeping
with Its franchise, then another
company stands ready to build It,
and the taxpayer is protected.


The water power company in it's
franchise agreed to put that bridge
into condition for street car pur.
poses. If that bridge could not be
made At, then, logically, they must
put one there that will be fit. This
they have refused to do, but will
